title: How financial stress quietly makes you poorer (and what you can do about it)
description: BUSINESS NEWS - A lot of people think that...
bodyText:
[{"type":"paragraph","text":"Sponsored Content","html":"Sponsored Content"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"BUSINESS NEWS - A lot of people think that financial stress is only an emotional issue that makes you fight with your partner or lose sleep.","html":"BUSINESS NEWS - A lot of people think that financial stress is only an emotional issue that makes you fight with your partner or lose sleep."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"But here's something you might not know: stress doesn't simply make you feel awful; it also makes you poorer."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"If you're worried about money, you're more likely to live. If you worry too much about little things that are happening right now, you stop seeing the broader picture, make bad decisions, and miss out on opportunities. This creates a fatal cycle over time: stress encourages people to make bad money decisions, which makes them even more stressed.","html":"If you're worried about money, you're more likely to live. If you worry too much about little things that are happening right now, you stop seeing the broader picture, make bad decisions, and miss out on opportunities. This creates a fatal cycle over time: stress encourages people to make bad money decisions, which makes them even more stressed."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The brain on money stressWhen you worry about money, your brain releases cortisol—the “fight or flight” hormone. That’s great if you need to run from a tiger, but terrible for investing or saving. High cortisol levels push you to seek quick rewards and avoid risks at all costs.","html":"The brain on money stress
When you worry about money, your brain releases cortisol—the “fight or flight” hormone. That’s great if you need to run from a tiger, but terrible for investing or saving. High cortisol levels push you to seek quick rewards and avoid risks at all costs."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"For example, stressed investors often panic-sell during market dips or keep all their cash in a savings account “just to feel safe.” The irony? By trying to protect their money, they lose to inflation and miss out on compounding growth.","html":"For example, stressed investors often panic-sell during market dips or keep all their cash in a savings account “just to feel safe.” The irony? By trying to protect their money, they lose to inflation and miss out on compounding growth."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This is where education and structured learning can change everything. Taking something like Indices Trading Courses teaches you to look at the market from a big-picture perspective. Indices are less volatile than single stocks, and understanding them can help calm that fear-driven urge to constantly “do something” with your investments.","html":"This is where education and structured learning can change everything. Taking something like Indices Trading Courses teaches you to look at the market from a big-picture perspective. Indices are less volatile than single stocks, and understanding them can help calm that fear-driven urge to constantly “do something” with your investments."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"A recent study from the National Endowment for Financial Education (NEFE) found that people with higher financial literacy experience significantly less money-related stress and are 3 times more likely to stick to long-term financial plans","html":"A recent study from the National Endowment for Financial Education (NEFE) found that people with higher financial literacy experience significantly less money-related stress and are 3 times more likely to stick to long-term financial plans"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The hidden costs of stress-based decisionsYou might think, “Okay, I’m stressed, but at least I’m not losing money.” Wrong. Stress makes you leak money in ways you don’t even notice:","html":"The hidden costs of stress-based decisions
You might think, “Okay, I’m stressed, but at least I’m not losing money.” Wrong. Stress makes you leak money in ways you don’t even notice:"},{"type":"list","items":["Impulse Spending – Buying things to feel better (“retail therapy”) adds up fast.","Avoiding Investments – Keeping all your money in cash because the market feels “too scary.”","Paying for Convenience: You get takeout instead of cooking because you're too tired to make dinner.","Not taking advantage of chances because you think you're \"too busy to deal with it,\" like sales, better employment offers, or clever investments.","High-Interest Debt: People who are stressed out often don't pay their bills on time or use credit cards just to get by, which makes their debt worse."],"ordered":true},{"type":"paragraph","text":"These small mistakes will cost you a lot of money in the long run.","html":"These small mistakes will cost you a lot of money in the long run."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"How to stop the cycle and stop worrying about money","html":"How to stop the cycle and stop worrying about money"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The good news is? You can teach your brain not to make money decisions based on fear. This is how:","html":"The good news is? You can teach your brain not to make money decisions based on fear. This is how:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"1. Make Everything AutomaticSet up automatic transfers to save or invest money. You should try not to rely on willpower too much.","html":"1. Make Everything Automatic
Set up automatic transfers to save or invest money. You should try not to rely on willpower too much."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"2. Learn first, then actWhen you learn, your fear goes away. You will feel better if you learn more about how the market works. You can do this by reading books, taking classes, or even just reading fair financial blogs.","html":"2. Learn first, then act
When you learn, your fear goes away. You will feel better if you learn more about how the market works. You can do this by reading books, taking classes, or even just reading fair financial blogs."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"3. Focus on Systems, Not ResultsInstead of obsessing over “Did I make money this month?”, focus on sticking to a strategy. Long-term investors win because they let systems run, not because they chase every market move.","html":"3. Focus on Systems, Not Results
Instead of obsessing over “Did I make money this month?”, focus on sticking to a strategy. Long-term investors win because they let systems run, not because they chase every market move."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"4. Practice “Financial Mindfulness”Before any money decision, pause for 24 hours. Question yourself: Do I do this because it makes sense or because I'm scared? You may be shocked at how often you let your feelings show.","html":"4. Practice “Financial Mindfulness”
Before any money decision, pause for 24 hours. Question yourself: Do I do this because it makes sense or because I'm scared? You may be shocked at how often you let your feelings show."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"5. Start saving for an emergencyWhen you know you have enough money to cover your bills for three to six months, you can make smart, long-term choices.","html":"5. Start saving for an emergency
When you know you have enough money to cover your bills for three to six months, you can make smart, long-term choices."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Why calm investors winLook at the world’s most successful investors. They are not the ones who look at charts all day; they are the ones who stay cool when everyone else goes crazy.\"Be afraid when others are greedy and greedy when others are afraid,\" Warren Buffett said. You can only accomplish that if you can deal with your own stress.","html":"Why calm investors win
Look at the world’s most successful investors. They are not the ones who look at charts all day; they are the ones who stay cool when everyone else goes crazy.\"Be afraid when others are greedy and greedy when others are afraid,\" Warren Buffett said. You can only accomplish that if you can deal with your own stress."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Not worrying about money makes you feel better and helps you make better decisions about how to spend it, keep it safe, and let your savings grow.","html":"Not worrying about money makes you feel better and helps you make better decisions about how to spend it, keep it safe, and let your savings grow."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Last thoughtsFinancial stress is more than just a feeling—it’s an invisible tax on your wealth. Every panic-driven decision, every impulse purchase, every delayed investment is money quietly slipping away.","html":"Last thoughts
Financial stress is more than just a feeling—it’s an invisible tax on your wealth. Every panic-driven decision, every impulse purchase, every delayed investment is money quietly slipping away."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The solution isn’t to magically “stop worrying.” It’s to build systems, learn consistently, and shift your focus to long-term strategies. Because when your mind is calm, your money finally has a chance to work for you.","html":"The solution isn’t to magically “stop worrying.” It’s to build systems, learn consistently, and shift your focus to long-term strategies. Because when your mind is calm, your money finally has a chance to work for you."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: How financial stress quietly makes you poorer (and what you can do about it)
